LAW & MOTION, DEPT. 302, AS TO THE OCT-23-2020 HEARING RE: CROSS-DEFENDANT STELLA CHEN'S DEMURRER TO CROSS-COMPLAINT, THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING. CROSS-DEFENDANT STELLA CHEN'S DEMURRERS TO CROSS-COMPLAINTS ARE ORDERED OFF CALENDAR FOR FAILURE TO COMPLY WITH CCP 430.41. THAT LAW REQUIRES A "MEET AND CONFER IN PERSON OR BY TELEPHONE" BEFORE A DEMURRER MAY BE FILED AND COUNSEL'S DECLARATION INDICATES THAT HAS NOT OCCURRED. THE PARTIES ARE ORDERED TO MEET AND CONFER IMMEDIATELY. CROSS-DEFENDANT CHEN'S RESPONSE TO THE CROSS-COMPLAINT IS NOW DUE BY NOVEMBER 23, 2020. JUDGE: RICHARD B. Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss Thai Ming Chius Cross-Complaint
Off Calendar Feb-02-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances, tentative ruling adopted as follows: CROSS-DEFENDANT STELLA CHEN'S NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION TO DISMISS THAI MING CHIU'S CROSS-COMPLAINT is DENIED. The Court notes that Fu's bankruptcy and judge Kahn's October 30, 2018 are not addressed in the moving papers. For October 30, 2018 hearing judge Kahn issued a tentative ruling stating in relevant part: "If plaintiff Tony Fu's bankruptcy case is still pending and if Mr. Fu fraudulently transferred assets, wouldn't those assets be part of his bankruptcy estate? Counsel should be prepared to address that question at the hearing" After the hearing, Judge Kahn ruled: "Off calendar, the Court lacks authority based on an automatic stay. The moving party may bring another demurrer in the future when the case is no longer stayed." According to the Court's records' no demurrer was filed and no timely challenge was made to this determination and the issue of the Court's "erring" in tis ruling was no raised by Chen until Reply to this motion was filed on January 25, 2023, 4+ years later, arguing that "[f]rankly, the Court was wrong in taking the motion off calendar, as there was no automatic stay in place." Per the Court's October 30, 2018 ruling, this case was stayed due to Fu's bankruptcy. No argument is made in the moving papers regarding the timeline of such bankruptcy or Chen's attempts (the moving party on the demurrer) to address the Court's ruling Chen believed to be "erroneous;" or any authority for the proposition that it was Chiu's burden to address the ruling which Chen believed to be "erroneous." Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: J. Murphy; Not Reported. (D501)
